reviews.quote_short: Gypsy Pistoleros are a serious proposition. Dark Faerie Tales is their fourth album in five years, and it's the best of the lot.
reviews.quote_long: More obvious is that the band's sixth album is a thoroughly convincing excursion to their genre-fluid realm of rock. None of Dark Faerie Tales' eclecticism feels tagged on. Rather, it's an accomplished mutation.

reviews.quote_short: This florid, fecund foursome are just brilliant, always have been.
Gypsy Pistoleros deliver everything they promise and more.
reviews.quote_long: If a world was ever ready for an album like this, then this is the time. Gypsy Pistoleros have made an album that knows who you are and how you feel, with songs that reach corners of the mind you may have not known existed until now.

reviews.quote_short: Once upon a time there was a band called Gypsy Pistoleros. They were one of the best bands in Britain. Dark Faerie Tales underlines it again.
reviews.quote_long: That, frankly, is where this thing lives. In the lip curl. In the swagger. In the fact it never walks when it can strut, never struts when it can stagger glam-drunk through a hook, and never settles for being one song when it can be about four at once.

reviews.quote_short: Feral, glitter-streaked, gothic punk rock with teeth — a riot for anyone bored of beige rock.
reviews.quote_long: In 2026, while much of rock music plays it safe, Gypsy Pistoleros are burning the rulebook. This is not nostalgia. This is not cosplay. This is fur-clad glam decadence colliding with gothic ritual, punk fury, and sleazy rock 'n' roll danger.

reviews.quote_short: A triumphant 10/10, confirming that the band has not only met but exceeded expectations.
reviews.quote_long: Solidifying Gypsy Pistoleros' reputation as genre innovators and making Dark Faerie Tales a must-listen for those seeking authentic, boundary-pushing rock.

reviews.quote_short: Bigger, darker, bolder and completely without compromise.
reviews.quote_long: Dark Faerie Tales is the moment everything Gypsy Pistoleros have ever been finally collides into one fully realised identity. The chaos is still there, but now it's controlled; the madness remains, but now it has purpose.

reviews.quote_short: A litany of exquisite strut, of unboastful cool which dramatically rocks and shakes the soul with fury and pleasure in equal parts.
reviews.quote_long: Dark Faerie Tales is the superb follow up to the Church Of The Pistoleros, and those in the packed pews of the cathedral being built on incredible foundations, and the praise be's are loud and bountiful.

reviews.quote_short: One of a kind and have stood widely aside of the crowd for a fair while now — a gloriously twisted and exhilarating adventure.
reviews.quote_long: Gypsy Pistoleros is one of those wonderful oddities; a band bearing a thickly recognisable voice and creative breath that is solely theirs yet with every release they still unveil something bold and unique within that familiarity.

reviews.quote_short: Rock music in 2026 may play it safe, but the Gypsy Pistoleros are burning the rule book, and Dark Faerie Tales is the fire. This is the real deal.
reviews.quote_long: A love letter to the beautiful and the broken, scrawled in lipstick on a cracked mirror in a green room that's a little more squalid, a little more glamorous, than we can quite define.

reviews.quote_short: A love letter to the beautiful and broken, a twisted hymn for the strange of heart, and a reminder that rock 'n' roll still has room for danger, drama and glitter in the dark.
reviews.quote_long: Gypsy Pistoleros return with their most fully realised statement yet. A fairytale for the damned filled with outsiders, night-dwellers and romantics with chipped teeth.

reviews.quote_short: Wild, unpredictable, and perfectly encapsulates a band that is entirely, unapologetically themselves. The Gypsy Pistoleros are back, armed with the best album of their career.
Loud, dramatic, and gloriously unhinged. Gypsy Pistoleros are back, and they still sound like nobody else.
reviews.quote_long: Darker and more theatrical than Church of the Pistoleros, it blends sleazy punk energy with gothic drama and flashes of fire.

reviews.quote_short: The band's upcoming album could well be the genre's modern-day Permission to Land. I love it.
reviews.quote_long: There's a little symphonic metal here, a lot of glam rock and total musical mayhem.

reviews.quote_short: A viable rallying point, a force for hope in dark and worrying times. Get this record as soon as you can.
reviews.quote_long: Gypsy Pistoleros no longer seems like a half-baked gimmick. Not many bands have the strength of character to take on such a mantle, but on the evidence of their last two records I'm starting to think the Pistoleros might just be such an outfit.

reviews.quote_short: Dripping in sleaze-glam attitude and a kind of ragged grandeur that feels equal parts back-alley punk show and end-of-the-world cabaret. Consider us fully spellbound.
Gypsy Pistoleros have conjured up an epic album, which more than lives up to its promises. Join them on this wild, strange, emotional musical rollercoaster!
Rock 'n' roll is alive, dangerous, and wearing glitter.
reviews.quote_long: Beautifully feral, unpredictable, and fiercely independent. They do not just play a gig, they host an immersive, theatrical rock 'n' roll ritual that demands your full attention.

reviews.quote_short: Hallowed be their name!
A sonic force. No other band comes close.
The Church needs more sinners.
Absolutely smashed it!
This is the Church in which to be bound.
A rallying cry for misfits everywhere.
